tool.name: nthseq
tool.summary: Select one sequence record by 1-based ordinal position

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `nthseq` selects exactly one sequence record from a local sequence stream by ordinal position. The tool reuses the shared EMBOSS-RS sequence loader and typed single-record result path instead of re-parsing format-specific content inside the command.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts one local sequence input path plus one selection index. The index is **1-based** and must refer to an existing record in the loaded input stream.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one selected sequence record through the shared FASTA output path. CLI output also includes the standard EMBOSS-RS method summary lines reporting the input path, selected index, total record count, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs nthseq`. Validation currently covers first, interior, and last-record selection plus empty-input, malformed-input, and out-of-range failure behavior.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release supports ordinal selection only. Identifier-based selection, multiple returned records, and grouped reporting remain deferred. Duplicate identifiers do not affect behavior because selection is position-based.

artifacts.label: Three-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed multi-record FASTA fixture used to validate stable ordinal selection.

examples.title: Select the second record from a three-record FASTA input
examples.description: Selects the middle record from a small FASTA fixture and returns only that sequence record.

expected_outputs.label: Selected sequence record
expected_outputs.description: A FASTA sequence record containing the second record from the source fixture.
